unsupported_device.title: Unsupported EcoFlow device ({prefix})
unsupported_device.description: An EcoFlow device with serial prefix `{prefix}` is bound to your account but is not supported yet by this integration, so no entities were created for it. Only the prefix is shown for privacy. Please open an issue on the project's GitHub repository including this prefix so support can be added.

user.title: EcoFlow IoT
user.description: Enter the Access Key and Secret Key from the EcoFlow developer portal (developer.ecoflow.com → Security).

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate EcoFlow IoT
reauth_confirm.description: Your EcoFlow API credentials are no longer valid. Enter updated keys.

error.invalid_auth: Invalid Access Key or Secret Key.
error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the EcoFlow API.

data.reset_grid_energy: Reset grid import/export energy totals & history

data_description.poll_interval: How often to poll the HTTP API (fallback when MQTT is unavailable, and the cadence for refreshing HTTP-only values).
data_description.mqtt_stale_seconds: If no MQTT message arrives within this window, the integration polls HTTP.
data_description.invert_grid_sign: Some Stream units report grid power with the sign inverted — they show power fed into the grid as positive instead of negative, the opposite of Home Assistant's convention. This depends on the model and firmware, so it can't be detected automatically. Leave this on (the default) and the sign is corrected: Grid power reads negative while you export, and the Grid import / export energy sensors count in the right direction. Turn it off only if your unit already matches Home Assistant — i.e. exporting to the grid already shows Grid power as negative (import positive, export negative). If you change this setting, also tick "Reset grid import/export energy totals" below — the existing totals were built up in the wrong direction and won't correct themselves.
data_description.reset_grid_energy: One-shot action: tick this and save to fully reset the Grid import energy and Grid export energy sensors. It zeroes the live counters and deletes their recorded history and Energy Dashboard statistics, so the old wrong-direction data is gone and they start fresh from zero. Recommended after changing the sign setting above. Leave it off for normal use.
